title: strychnos xylophylla
intro: Strychnos xylophylla, also known as the West African poison nut, is a plant species within the Strychnaceae family, characterized by its toxic seeds and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been linked to various health benefits such as pain relief and wound healing. In herbal preparations, the seeds and roots of Strychnos xylophylla are often used to create remedies for various ailments, including fever reduction and digestive issues. However, due to its toxic nature, the plant should be handled with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional or experienced herbalist.
